title: Rebuild Really New Fan Accessible
wikitext:
[]: Rebuild-Really New Fan Accessible? [SPOILERS]             Quote Post by Gamer137 » Thu Jul 22, 2010 12:23 am I'm sure most of us are aware by now that Anno states these movies are intended to be both open and accessible to new fans as well as satisfying to old ones. While the latter is for another thread, is the former really all that true? Now a possible 7.5 hours worth of movies is not going to compared to 13 hours and an extra 1.5 which the original series gave us. This means there is much less material to uncover and have to tell. While I can see simplifying the complexity as a noble endeavor, is it still too much for four movies to handle? A few hours ago I had just finished watching 2.22 after receiving it yesterday in the mail. I also watched it back-to-back with 1.11, and I could not for the life of me understand the delivery. Throughout both movies, I kept relying on my already established view points on the series because everything feels so rushed. I did almost shed a tear when Asuka was allegedly killed in Unit 03, but I can almost guarantee you a new fan will not become attached enough to the character to feel much of anything during that fight. Asuka's dislike for Rei also comes in really fast before you can learn how much she detests doll-like people and objects. Now while I am not providing the best examples in the world, I do feel while simplicity is nice, the delivery of it is so rushed I don't see new fans being able to put the pieces together effectively and I would recommend they watch the series first instead. So are these movies really new fan friendly? Also, don't buy Hong Kong versions of movies without handling broken English. The subtitles have obvious errors by the truck load.

[]: Re: Rebuild-Really New Fan Accessible? [SPOILERS]             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Jul 22, 2010 1:09 am Gamer137 wrote: Asuka's dislike for Rei also comes in really fast because you can learn how much she detests doll-like people and objects.

[]: Re: Rebuild-Really New Fan Accessible? [SPOILERS]             Quote Post by Lucretius » Thu Jul 22, 2010 1:25 am Gamer137 wrote: So are these movies really new fan friendly?

[]: Re: Rebuild-Really New Fan Accessible? [SPOILERS]             Quote Post by Velefor » Thu Jul 22, 2010 1:33 am I agree with thread starter points. I too had a sensation of events being far too rushed, especially in 2.22. But I still believe that that the only way it could be filmed in order to tell the story in 90 minutes time span. Gamer137 wrote: So are these movies really new fan friendly?

[]: Re: Rebuild-Really New Fan Accessible? [SPOILERS]             Quote Post by Clover » Thu Jul 22, 2010 1:54 am Velefor wrote: At my place we will watch blue-ray versions of 1.11 and 2.22 on big screen LCD television and surround sound system. After that I will question each of them for feedback on the movies.

[]:             Quote Post by Velefor » Thu Jul 22, 2010 2:04 am Also, subs, how did you get them?

[]: Re: Rebuild-Really New Fan Accessible? [SPOILERS]             Quote Post by Gamer137 » Thu Jul 22, 2010 2:06 am Mr. Tines wrote: And then Shikinami spends time talking with a favoured old doll of hers...

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Jul 22, 2010 2:06 am Judging by reactions from new friends across the interwebs, I'd say yes. Whether they can get all the content from it is another thing entirely, but it's unreasonable to expect them to anyway since we're still trying to do that with the anime.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Thu Jul 22, 2010 3:11 am Well, i read some reviews spread across the internet, particularly MyAnimeList.net, and there were neophytes there who wrote "OMG best thing ever!!11!". So yeah, i guess Rebuild can entertain new audiences too, but i think that has more to do with the "Oooh, shiny!" effect than any real emotional involvement.

[]:             Quote Post by Ramiel » Thu Jul 22, 2010 3:18 am I'd say it's friendly to newcomers. They're not going to get all of the information they'd have if they'd actually watched NGE and EoE, but Rebuild is entertaining and not too confusing. Of course, there are things that won't entirely make sense yet, but there's still at least one more movie yet to be released (I'm crossing my fingers for two, but not getting my hopes up too high), so of course they can't fill in every gap. I mean, not everything in the original series was explained half way through, so not everything in Rebuild has to be in order to make it more newcomer friendly. ...where was I going with this again? Nya. I can give a definite answer when I make my friends watch the screening at Manifest. One of them saw NGE years and years ago, one's never really heard much about it and one is doing her best to avoid it solely because I talk too much about it.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Jul 22, 2010 7:00 am My stepsister watched the 1.0 dub with me and thought it was "interesting" (Annoooo~ ), she didn't see NGE before. If I get her or somebody else to watch 2.22, I'll report back. That said, I think yes, the films draw in newcomers. It's building up to enough mystery and action to keep them hooked. I had a pacing issuse with Misato & Shinji's car chat; wondered when Operation Yashima happened. (Even NGE had cleanup shots!) Besides that, 2.22 back-to-back with 1.11 wasn't jarring for me.

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Thu Jul 22, 2010 11:40 am my brother watched both movies with me last week, and he seemed to like them well enough. of course, he wasn't exactly trying to uncover the mysteries within (like i would be), he was just watching them to be entertained. but for what it's worth, he didn't seem confused at all.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Thu Jul 22, 2010 12:36 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Judging by reactions from new friends across the interwebs, I'd say yes.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Jul 28, 2010 5:39 pm Well, I showed 1.01 to my parents and Sister, and I didn't have to explain anything that wasn't in the movie itself, tough my parents were very convinced that Rei would kick the bucket any moment and were pretty surprised when she survived the first movie. I'll try out 2.22 on them as soon as I get it. They're making us poor folks in germany wait untill freakin' SEPTEMBER.

[]:             Quote Post by Gamer137 » Wed Jul 28, 2010 7:33 pm Kendrix wrote: Well, I showed 1.01 to my parents and Sister, and I didn't have to explain anything that wasn't in the movie itself, tough my parents were very convinced that Rei would kick the bucket any moment and were pretty surprised when she survived the first movie. I'll try out 2.22 on them as soon as I get it. They're making us poor folks in germany wait untill freakin' SEPTEMBER.

[]:             Quote Post by Ardorius » Wed Jul 28, 2010 10:37 pm I was lucky enough to see 1.0 in an arthouse theater last summer. I was so excited to see it on the big screen that I babbled to my SO about for at least a week prior. He had never seen the series, and knew nothing about it, but he was willing to go with me to the theater. After the end credits rolled, on our way back to the car, he started asking me questions about what this, that, or the other thing meant, and then actually asked me if we could watch the series. (He's not really into anime, either) Cut to about a year later, and he's been completely addicted. He's watched the series and EoE several times through, and is eagerly awaiting a domestic release of 2.0 and of course 2.22. Basically, his experience at the theater as a neophyte got him "into" Evangelion. So I would have to say yes, that it is newbie friendly.

[]:             Quote Post by facespace » Wed Jul 28, 2010 11:45 pm I saw Eva 1.0 at a film festival in April of 2009, I honestly believed it was an abbreviated version of the first six episodes. About 6 months ago I showed it to my friends when I got it on DVD. They had never seen the show, but they thought it was pretty good (these people weren't really into anime but they enjoyed it a lot). I think that the second film is not as good to introduce to new viewers, because the addition (and subtraction) of characters would leave them a bit confused. I realize that 2.0 is really detouring from the original story, which I like a lot more, but the way the second film was executed put viewers into the action too quickly without giving them enough time to get into the characters.
